items.title: Use bootstrap + feature subsampling together
items.avoid: Disabling bootstrap (bootstrap=False) and max_features=1.0 unless you deliberately want a different ensemble variant.
items.prefer: Keep bootstrap=True and set max_features to 'sqrt' (classification) or ~p/3 (regression) as a starting point.
items.why: Both row-level and feature-level randomness are what make Breiman's random forest more than plain bagging.
items.example: RandomForestClassifier(max_features='sqrt', bootstrap=True, n_estimators=200) is the sklearn default recipe.

items.title: Tune depth and max_features on validation folds
items.avoid: Fixing max_depth=None and never checking whether a single tree is memorizing within each bootstrap.
items.prefer: Cross-validate max_depth, min_samples_leaf, and max_features; use OOB score as a quick sanity check.
items.why: Forests average variance, but each base tree can still be too flexible for tiny bootstrap samples.
items.example: GridSearchCV over max_depth ∈ {None, 10, 20} and max_features ∈ {'sqrt', 0.5} with 5-fold CV.

items.title: Scale n_estimators until OOB or CV plateaus
items.avoid: Stopping at n_estimators=10 because training is fast, leaving high variance on the table.
items.prefer: Increase trees until OOB/CV metric stabilizes—often 100–500 for medium tabular sets.
items.why: Variance reduction comes from averaging more decorrelated trees; too few trees underuse the ensemble.
items.example: Plot oob_score_ vs n_estimators in steps of 50; stop when gains fall below 0.001 accuracy.
